Details: Ricin inactivation by chlorine in water can be very effective at high doses and long contact times not typical of drinking water treatment.  Inactivation values in excess of 99% were observed for chlorine dosages that ranged from 100 to 223 mg/L and contact times ranging from 20 to 2,880 minutes. A chlorine dose of 5 to 10 mg/L and 20 to 30 minute contact times were ineffective in controlling ricin.
